title: Pull Requests and Code Review
description:
## The Unit of Collaboration

Git hosting platforms such as **GitHub**, **GitLab**, and **Bitbucket** add collaboration features on top of plain Git. Central among them is the **pull request** (called a **merge request** on GitLab).

What is the primary purpose of a pull/merge request?

options.text: It compresses the repository to save storage space on the server.
It proposes merging one branch into another, providing a place for code review and a gate where automated checks (CI) must pass before merging.
It is the only way to clone a repository to your local machine.
It permanently locks a branch so no one else can commit to the project.

explanation: A **pull request** (PR) / **merge request** (MR) proposes integrating the commits from one branch (e.g. a feature branch) into another (e.g. `main`). It creates a focused space for **code review** — teammates comment on the diff, request changes, and approve — and it serves as a **gate** where CI pipelines (tests, linting, builds) run automatically; branch protection rules can require those checks and approvals to pass before the merge is allowed. Cloning (c) uses `git clone` and needs no PR; PRs neither compress storage (a) nor lock the whole project (d).

hints.title: Why do teams require PRs?
hints.body: Think about the two things teams want to happen automatically before code reaches main: human review and passing automated checks.
